commands.review_summary:
The PR adds cooperative yielding and abort checks while draining dense native Ollama NDJSON stream chunks, and adds regression tests for event-loop progress plus abort classification.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +86. Total +133 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level for this PR's narrow bug: current main drains dense parsed Ollama stream chunks without an explicit timer yield, and the added regression targets that path. The full Windows live reproduction from the linked issue was not rerun in this review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair target is visible; the next action is maintainer landing judgment on the protected Ollama mitigation and its live-proof gap.

target.title: fix(ollama): yield during dense stream processing
target.body:
## Summary

- rebase the Ollama dense stream cooperative-yield fix onto current maintainer work, preserving @udaymanish6 as commit author
- yield and check aborts while draining dense native Ollama NDJSON chunks so one large read cannot monopolize the Gateway event loop
- preserve caller cancellation semantics during dense stream processing and add regression coverage for timer progress plus abort classification

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.
Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 as the rebased maintainer branch.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed on final head `3023487b5ae`.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts` - passed on final head `3023487b5ae`.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts` - passed on final head `3023487b5ae`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts --reporter=dot` - 1 file / 12 tests passed on final head `3023487b5ae`.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean; no accepted/actionable findings; `overall: patch is correct (0.86)`.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate after the latest rebase: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 180m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`; provider `aws`, lease `cbx_64545702c600`, slug `blue-barnacle`, run `run_4d4493d6b83c`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit `0`, `leaseStopped=true`, lanes `extensions`, `extensionTests`, base `7bf6667d6c8`.
- Earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate on the prior current base also passed: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_3b9111cb9846`, slug `harbor-crayfish`, run `run_085a4c81938f`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit `0`, `leaseStopped=true`, lanes `extensions`, `extensionTests`.
- Local Ollama live check was not available in this environment: `ollama` CLI unavailable and `http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags` not reachable.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: dense native Ollama stream chunks now cooperatively yield during parsing instead of draining an entire large read in one synchronous loop, while caller aborts during the drain surface as `aborted` rather than provider `error`.
Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a linked worktree, formatting/lint checks, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: See the Verification section above.
Evidence after fix: refreshed head `3023487b5ae`; focused stream tests passed with the dense-yield and abort regressions; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed the extension and extension test lanes after the latest rebase.
Observed result after fix: dense stream unit coverage confirms timer progress during parser drain, aborts scheduled before the first cooperative yield are observed before the stream can finish, aborts during dense processing keep `reason` and assistant `stopReason` set to `aborted`, and changed extension type/lint/import-cycle guards passed remotely.
What was not tested: true live Ollama runtime was not available in this local environment; Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. This is one mitigation in the broader event-loop starvation cluster, not the final issue closeout by itself.
